The MI-D hashing function outputs 2 bits index, this is the EID of
the MI-D that corresponds to the index.

reg.doc:
the SLC hashing can be overridden by configuration.
The SAM of the source MI can be set to targets a single SLC

and will assign a new EID based on slc_hashing_override_value register

the MI-D hashing can be overridden by configuration.

field.name: value

field.name: eid

field.doc:
Set if the RQM can send flits to this EID on this VC.
e.g. If the RQM is in MI-C, en shall be 0 for all SLCs that are not
in the same NUMA cluster as this MI-C
field.name: en

reg.name: cfg_rqm_initial_e2e_credits_sn

field.doc: Maximum credits that P0+P1 VC can consume together
field.name: total_credits

field.doc:
If set, CBusy is based on the maximum CBusy in the sliding window.
Otherwise CBusy is based on a factored average of the sliding window
field.name: max_out

field.doc:
Set to value between 3 and 7.
WinSize sets the size of the sliding window to 2^WinSize entries.
field.name: window_size

field.doc: Average factor for the CBusy. BusyFactor is set in U1.7 fixed point.
field.name: busy_factor

field.doc: Aging interval of the sliding window. If set to 0, the aging mechanism is disabled
field.name: aging_period

field.doc:
If set, CBusy is based on the maximum CBusy in the sliding window.
Otherwise CBusy is based on a factored average of the sliding window
field.name: max_out

field.doc:
Set to value between 3 and 7.
WinSize sets the size of the sliding window to 2^WinSize entries.
field.name: window_size

field.doc: Average factor for the CBusy. BusyFactor is set in U1.7 fixed point.
field.name: busy_factor

field.doc: Aging interval of the sliding window. If set to 0, the aging mechanism is disabled
field.name: aging_period

field.doc:
If set, the RQM uses indications from the MI-F for CBusy calculation.
Should be disabled in MIs connected to a specific NUMA domain. (e.g. MI-C)
field.name: window

field.doc:
To allow the reordering at the destination each PCIe peer-to-peer request is assigned with PCIe ID
noting the source of the request and a sequence number for each request.
The target MI-F of each flits is set by this lookup table, the input of the table is
{SQN, TargetPCIe_id[3:0]} and the output is the EID of the target.
If the PCIe target is in a remote die, the LUT is used and the output of the
LUT is the EID of the target MI-F
field.name: lut

field.doc:
The threshold that determines the TableIndex which is used as an
index to a lookup table of the target MI-F EID.
The table index is calulated as follows (A = {SLC[0],SLC[6:2]}) :
if A<TH0: TableIndex = 0
elif A<TH1: TableIndex =1
elif A<TH2: TableIndex = 2
elif A<TH3: TableIndex = 3
elif A<TH4: TableIndex = 4
elif A<TH5: TableIndex = 5
elif A<TH6: TableIndex = 6
elif A<TH7: TableIndex = 7
else: TableIndex = 8
The thresholds TH0-TH7 shall be set according to the following table and the number of
active MI-Fs:
TH Idx   Eight Active MI-Fs      Nine Active MI-Fs
0                 7                    6
1                 14                   12
2                 21                   19
3                 28                   25
4                 39                   35
5                 46                   41
6                 53                   48
7                 60                   54
field.name: threshold

field.doc:
A lookup table of the target MI-F EID, the index to the lookup table
is the table index calculated based on cfg_rqm_target_mif_table_index_threshold.
The lookup table should be configured with the active MI-F EID in order from left
to right. The EID of the leftmost active MI-F should be written to the first entry
of the table (MI-F0 EID). If there are 9 active MI-Fs then the EID of the
rightmost active MI-F is written to the last entry (MI-F8 EID) and if there are 8
active MI-F the EID of the rightmost active MI-F is written to the MI-F7 EID entry.
field.name: eid

field.doc:
If enabled, target EID of PCIe remote memory access is calculated by MI-F hashing.
If not enabled, the EID is taken from the Direct EID of the SAM region
field.name: en

reg.doc:
The RQM can optionally drop PrefetchTgt requests according to the CBusy state.
The RQM holds a configurable CBusy threshold and if this option is enabled by
software PrefetchTgt requests are dropped by the RQM if the CBusy value is
equal to or greater than the CBusy threshold

field.doc:
If set, the RQM drops PrefetchTgt requests if the CBusy value is
equal to or greater than cfg_rqm_cbusy_drop_prefetch_tgt.value
field.name: en

field.doc:
The CBusy threshold, if the CBusy value is equal to or greater than this threshold
and cfg_rqm_cbusy_drop_prefetch_tgt_en, The RQM drops PrefetchTgt requests
field.name: value

reg.name: cfg_rqm_cbusy_drop_prefetch_tgt

reg.doc: Option to override the request src_numa_id

field.doc:
If set, the RQM will override the src_numa_id and will assign
the src_numa_id field based on cfg_rqm_src_numa_id_override.value register
field.name: en

field.doc:
The value of the src_numa_id to use in case
cfg_rqm_src_numa_id_override.en is set
field.name: value

reg.name: cfg_rqm_src_numa_id_override

field.doc: The enumerate ID of the Default MI-A that serves as the completer for unmapped transactions
field.name: default_mia

field.doc: The enumerate ID of the DVM hub
field.name: dvm_hub

field.doc: Weight of each VC in the VC weighted round robin arbiter.
field.name: weight

field.doc:
If a bit is set, a core request to SLC is transmitted with P1 VC.
The MSB of the opcode is not used by
the core so we need only NUM_REQ_OPCODES/2 (128) bits.
By default the opcodes that are p1 are :
ReadShared
ReadClean
ReadOnce
ReadNoSnp
ReadUnique
ReadNoSnpSep
ReadOnceCleanInvalid
ReadOnceMakeInvalid
ReadNotSharedDirty
CleanUnique
MakeUnique
MakeReadUnique
The bitmap[reg_index][bitmap_j] corresponds to 32 * reg_index + bitmap_j
field.name: bitmap

field.doc:
Bitmap of the opcodes that expected to be transmitted with p0 or p1 VC.
In case we get an expected p0/p1 opcode not to SLC, SAM error will be asserted
field.name: bitmap

field.doc:
Each bit corresponds to the request opcode enum.
If a bit is set, request to High Bandwidth MI-P will be directed to
EID = direct_eid (as configured in the relevant sam region).
If a bit is 0, request to High Bandwidth MI-P will be directed
to EID = (direct_eid + 1)
The bitmap[reg_index][bitmap_j] corresponds to 32 * reg_index + bitmap_j
field.name: bitmap

field.doc:
If set, the RQM will override the input flit's color
and will assign a new color based on override_color_value register
field.name: en

doc.content: The value of the color to use in case override_color_en is set

field.doc:
The base address of the fabric config space. 1MB granule (bits [47:20])
Shall match the SAM configurtion - base address for config region
field.name: base_address

field.doc:
The CCR is set with base EID pointing to the EID of the first agent in the CCR and is incremented with the
address offset. 1MB granule (bits [47:20]).
Base address of a CCR region must be aligned with the size of the CCR region.
Shall match the SAM configurtion - start address for the corresponding CCR region.
CCR0 supports up to 128 agents of 1MiB each. CCR0 address range is 128MiB.
CCR1 supports up to 16 agents of 1MiB each. CCR1 address range is 16MiB.
CCR2 supports up to 32 agents of 16MiB each. CCR2 address range is 512MiB.
field.name: base_address

field.doc:
The base address of the core config space. 1MB granule (bits [47:20])
Shall be aligned to 128MiB.
Shall match the SAM configurtion - base address for CCR region
field.name: base_address

doc.content: The Numa domain topology of the chiplet the RQM reside in
The indication regarding the die rotation

field.doc:
The chiplet ID the RQM reside in. chiplet 0 contains
Numas 0 & 1 (if exist) and chiplet 1 contains Numas 2 & 3 (if exist)
field.name: id

section.doc:
The RQM SAM region configuration.
For the RQM to be functional, the SAM must be configured.
The SAM can be configured even during ACTIVE state, after config_done has been asserted.
To configure the SAM after config_done has been asserted, SW needs to:
1.Ensure there are no more transactions to this region.
2.Disable the region.
3.Config the region
4.Enable the region
section.name: rqm_sam_region_regfile

field.doc:
SAM Region Start Address, 1MB granule (bits [47:20])
In the design, the 20 LSBs of the request address are asssumed to be 0x7FFFF.
SAM region is defined by cfg_sam_region_start[27:0] <= chi_req.addr[47:20] <= cfg_sam_region_end[27:0]
field.name: address

field.doc:
SAM Region End Address, 1MB granule (bits [47:20])
In the design, the 20 LSBs of the request address are asssumed to be 0x7FFFF.
SAM region is defined by cfg_sam_region_start[27:0] <= chi_req.addr[47:20] <= cfg_sam_region_end[27:0]
field.name: address

field.doc: The NUMA ID of a region
field.name: numa_id

field.doc: The target is High Bandwidth MI-P
field.name: is_miphb

field.doc:
SAM Region Enumerate ID. Applicable only if target type is set to MI, RMem, PConfig or PMem.
Otherwise the target EID will be calculated by the hashing function or
configuration logic.
field.name: direct_eid

field.doc:
The target is CXL.mem device (no need to hash over MI-Ds).
Relevant only in SLC System Address Mapping
field.name: cxl

field.doc: SAM Region Valid
field.name: en

field.doc:
The target PCIe id.
To allow the reordering at the destination each PCIe
peer-to-peer request is assigned with PCIe ID.
For PCIe located in chiplet 0, pcie_id MSB shall be 0.
For PCIe located in chiplet 1, pcie_id MSB shall be 1
field.name: id

section.doc:
For SLC :
We have 2 vectors, cfg_rate_limiter_regfile[0] is used when throttling_bit = 0,
cfg_rate_limiter_regfile[1] is used when throttling_bit = 1.
For Non SLC :
We have 12 vectors, and the vector we use is based on the CBusy and the req color.
Vectors mapping :
4'd0 - CBusy 00, Green Rate limiter vector.
4'd1 - CBusy 00, Yellow Rate limiter vector.
4'd2 - CBusy 00, Red Rate limiter vector.
4'd3 - CBusy 01, Green Rate limiter vector.
4'd4 - CBusy 01, Yellow Rate limiter vector.
4'd5 - CBusy 01, Red Rate limiter vector.
4'd6 - CBusy 10, Green Rate limiter vector.
4'd7 - CBusy 10, Yellow Rate limiter vector.
4'd8 - CBusy 10, Red Rate limiter vector.
4'd9 - CBusy 11, Green Rate limiter vector.
4'd10 - CBusy 11, Yellow Rate limiter vector.
4'd11 - CBusy 11, Red Rate limiter vector.
section.name: cfg_rate_limiter_regfile

field.doc:
Rate limiter bit map.
the limiter runs over these bits in a cycle,
enabling a request only when a bit is set (e.g. if we want a 50% rate limiter,
we will set only the even bits).
The bitmap[reg_index][bitmap_j] corresponds to 32 * reg_index + bitmap_j
field.name: vector
